mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE stays below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression, or single-series signals consistently outperform the joint specification.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate output and margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week despite higher interest rates.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening cycles, or INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in the prior quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer purchasing power, which flows through to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.4% month-over-month forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when inflation thresholds are breached, or the market prices in rate hikes without producing measurable equity drawdowns over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a synchronized demand and labor market slowdown, which reduces earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint effect is not statistically significant in walk-forward tests, or GDP deceleration alone predicts returns better than the interaction term across multiple market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth during periods of low unemployment reflects robust consumer spending that immediately boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or retail sales growth shows no predictive power for 5-day returns in high unemployment environments over a 3-year rolling window.
